description_long: This house features a steeply pitched, reddish-brown tiled gable roof with multiple intersecting sections and a prominent central chimney. The exterior walls are finished in a light cream color with dark wooden trim, evoking a Tudor Revival or half-timbered style. The structure includes a main two-story section, a smaller attached wing, and a stone-clad ground floor with an arched entryway. Windows are rectangular, symmetrically arranged, and framed with dark wood, while the roof includes a small dormer. The house sits atop a stone foundation, and the attached garage or side wing is roofed in the same tile as the main structure. The overall design suggests a suburban or historical urban neighborhood setting.
description: A two-story Tudor Revival-style house with a steep gable roof, cream walls, dark wood trim, and a stone foundation. Features include symmetrical windows, a central chimney, and an arched stone entryway.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main gable, central chimney, arched stone entryway, and attached garage wing.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the large gable end, symmetrical window arrangement, and the stone chimney.
description_view_2: Side view displaying the dormer window, extended wing, and the stone archway beneath the main structure.
description_view_3: Opposite side view focusing on the gable end, stone foundation, and a secondary entry or window at ground level.
attribute_entryway_design: Arched stone entryway with a recessed door, no porch, and dark wood trim.
